meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Client Workflow to Solve Basic App Bottlenecks
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'basic app' trap as a freelancer. Learn a high-speed client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver premium, scalable SaaS products.
content.answer_block: Break the 'basic app' ceiling by shifting from manual coding to an AI-orchestrated workflow. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you reduce development time by 70%, allowing you to deliver complex, high-margin enterprise features while maintaining total control over your production-ready codebase.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you’re trapped in a cycle of CRUD apps and simple landing pages. Every project feels like a grind, and your margins are shrinking as clients demand more complex features that your current manual workflow simply can't support. You're not just a developer; you're a bottleneck to your own potential.

But the game has changed. By leveraging an AI-first architecture, you can stop writing boilerplate and start engineering solutions. This isn't about letting AI 'do the work'—it's about becoming a high-leverage architect who orchestrates code at scale. Here is how you transition from a coder to a high-output AI Builder.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Define your high-level system requirements to generate a robust project structure that prevents technical debt before the first line of code is written.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready Next.js 14 architecture with Drizzle ORM, Shadcn/UI, and Clerk authentication. Include a modular folder structure that supports scalable server actions.

steps.title: Context Injection for Domain Logic
steps.description: Feed your specific client business logic into the environment so the AI understands the unique constraints of your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the attached business requirements document. Create a schema definition for the database that prioritizes multi-tenant data isolation.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build out discrete modules, ensuring each feature is tested and integrated into the scaffold.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the user dashboard component using the previously defined schema. Ensure all server-side actions are type-safe.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement user dashboard with server-side filtering and sorting'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Force the AI to review its own generated code against industry standards and security best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recently generated code for potential security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Propose refactoring for better memoization.

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Hand-off
steps.description: Configure the automated pipeline so you can ship updates with a single command, impressing clients with your speed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Vercel-compatible CI/CD pipeline that runs unit tests before deployment. Add a notification hook for Slack.

pro_tips: Always define a 'Global Style Guide' file in your root; AI models perform 3x better when they have a source of truth for design tokens.
Use Claude Code's '--compact' mode for small bug fixes to save token costs and maintain focus on specific files.
Never accept the first output; always use 'refine' prompts to enforce specific naming conventions that match your personal library.
Maintain a 'Knowledge Base' folder in your repo; feed it snippets of your best-performing past code to train the AI on your specific coding style.
Use Antigravity to generate 'Architecture Decision Records' (ADRs) so clients feel like they are paying for a professional engineering process, not just code.
content.next_steps: Start by auditing your last three projects. Identify the repetitive boilerplate you wrote and create a custom Antigravity template for it. Once your scaffold is ready, use Claude Code to migrate your current project into this new, optimized structure.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic apps and start building high-value systems that command premium rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
